title: Default module input ports not handled properly
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using the [test.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/4523054/test.zip) file below, containing both a verilog file and a SymbiYosys script, run SymbiYosys.

The key line at issue in the file is the input line of the submodule,
```
    input wire i_stall = 0;
```

If I understand how this is to work properly, `i_stall` should be set to zero only if it is not connected to any outside port.  Two tests are contained within the file--one that leaves this pin unconnected for which I expect the default to hold, and a second one that connects this pin to a top-level input for which I expect the default to be ignored.

Within the file, there are two properties.  The first checks that, if `i_stall` is left at its default, then the output should match a similar output that isn't constrained by `i_stall`.  The second property, a `cover()` statement, checks that `i_stall` can still be non-zero if included in a top level module input.

## Expected behavior

I expect both properties to pass--the assertion in the induction proof and the cover property when running cover.

## Actual behavior

When using Yosys from today's build, both properties fail.

When using the Symbiotic EDA Suite (commercial version) dated 2 March, 2020, the assertion succeeds bu the cover property fails.

Dan
